edits.rationale: Replaces modern idiomatic English with period-appropriate phrasing

edits.revised_text: through the gaps in the wooden shutters
edits.rationale: Window slats are anachronistic for 1st century Palestine

edits.original_text: he'd been living in for thirty-six years
edits.revised_text: he'd been living in for more than thirty years
edits.rationale: Ages weren't tracked with such precision in antiquity

edits.original_text: He'd woken in the dark. Not from a dream — from a sensation. The warmth beneath his ribs had shifted in the night, moved deeper, pressed against something it had never reached before.
edits.revised_text: He'd woken in the dark not from a dream but from a sensation—the warmth beneath his ribs shifting in the night, moving deeper, pressing against something it had never reached before.
edits.rationale: Combines choppy sentences for better rhythm and flow

edits.original_text: And the cascade had begun. Not the fragments he'd learned to manage — the scattered visions
edits.revised_text: The cascade erupted. Not the fragments he'd learned to manage — the scattered visions
edits.rationale: Cuts unnecessary 'began to' qualifier and uses stronger verb
edits.source_note: Line Editor - padding and word choice

edits.original_text: The bronze disc sat heavy in his palms, its geometric patterns catching the first grey light... The bronze disc was warm now... He held it against his chest and felt it pulse
edits.revised_text: The bronze disc sat heavy in his palms, its geometric patterns catching grey dawn light... Now warm against his chest, it pulsed with alien rhythm
edits.rationale: Consolidates repetitive phrasing about bronze and varies descriptive language

edits.revised_text: like storm fronts—sudden, devastating, beyond his control
edits.rationale: Replaces dead metaphor with fresher comparison

edits.original_text: I don't need the keys anymore
edits.revised_text: I no longer need the keys
edits.rationale: Eliminates modern contraction inappropriate for formal religious discourse

edits.original_text: leather scraps Simon had patched with fishing cord
edits.revised_text: leather scraps Simon had patched with hemp cord
edits.rationale: Fishing cord inappropriate for sandal repair; hemp more historically accurate

edits.original_text: The cloth fell away stiff with sweat and blood — six days of Helena's body pressed into the fabric.
edits.revised_text: The stiff cloth fell away, reeking of sweat and blood—six days of Helena's body had pressed the stench into every fiber.
edits.rationale: Converts passive voice to active for stronger narrative drive

edits.original_text: 'You're younger than I expected,' Helena said. 'You're more tired than you expected to be,' Mary answered.
edits.revised_text: 'You're younger than I expected.' 'You're more tired than you expected to be.'
edits.rationale: Trusts context to identify speakers in two-person exchange

edits.original_text: Jesus sat on the woven mat
edits.revised_text: Jesus sat upon his cloak spread over the stone ledge
edits.rationale: Stone ledges more typical for temporary shelter in Qumran caves than woven mats

edits.original_text: Jesus doubled over. A sound came out of him that Mary had never heard... Another life. Tobias, twenty generations back.
edits.revised_text: Jesus doubled over, that inhuman sound torn from his throat. Before the echo died, another life crashed into him. Tobias, twenty generations back.
edits.rationale: Adds bridging phrases between visions for smoother emotional build

edits.original_text: Sarah tugged Mary's sleeve. 'Mama, I can feel the pattern. The stories have two meanings.'
edits.revised_text: Sarah pressed closer to Mary's side, her small hand clutching Mary's robe. Her eyes were wide with understanding that she dared not voice aloud.
edits.rationale: A 7-year-old interrupting religious teaching would be inappropriate in 1st century Jewish culture
Uses more vivid verb instead of weak 'went'

edits.original_text: Jesus had been holding the Pattern Eye since before dawn, and his hands wouldn't stop shaking.
edits.revised_text: The forty-first generation was complete. Jesus held the Pattern Eye and felt twelve centuries of hunted ancestors breathing through his lungs—and knew he was the weapon Nefertari had theorized but never expected to exist.
edits.rationale: Stronger hook that immediately signals high stakes and unique premise

edits.original_text: He counted backward. Forty-one generations. From Nefertari's daughter to his mother. From his mother to him.
edits.revised_text: The numbers hit like a physical blow: forty-one unbroken generations. Every single ancestor a carrier. He wasn't just another teacher with fragmentary visions—he was the living completion of a twelve-century genetic weapon.
edits.rationale: Makes the revelation more explosive and emphasizes the high-concept hook

edits.original_text: Below them, Capernaum's lamps flickered on one by one. Nine months. And the Temple was already watching.
edits.revised_text: Below them, Capernaum's lamps flickered on one by one. Mary was about to speak when torchlight moved on the road—too organized, too purposeful for traders. 'Temple guards,' Jesus whispered. 'They're not waiting for morning.'
edits.rationale: Creates active cliffhanger instead of contemplative ending to drive thriller momentum
edits.source_note: Thread Tracker - thread closure and Acquisitions Editor - series potential
